classified_blocks.source_text_excerpt: Unit I: Matter-Nature and Behaviour
Definition of matter; Particulate Nature of Matter; States of Matter (solid, liquid, gas); change of state...
Elements, compounds and mixtures. Heterogeneous and homogenous mixtures, colloids and suspensions...
Physical and chemical changes; Atoms and molecules; Law of Chemical Combination...
Sub-atomic particles (Electrons, protons, neutrons); Models of atom; Valency...

classified_blocks.source_heading: Unit II: Organization in the Living World
classified_blocks.source_text_excerpt: Unit II: Organization in the Living World
Prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells; multicellular organisms; Cell membrane and cell wall...
Structure and functions of animal tissues (four types) and plant tissues...

classified_blocks.source_heading: Unit III: Motion, Force and Work
classified_blocks.source_text_excerpt: Unit III: Motion, Force and Work
Distance, displacement, velocity; uniform and non-uniform motion...
Newton's Laws of Motion; Action and Reaction forces...
Universal Law of Gravitation; Gravity...
Thrust and Pressure; Archimedes' Principle...
Work done by a Force; Energy, power...
Nature of sound; propagation in various media...
Unit IV: Food Production

classified_blocks.source_heading: Plant and animal breeding and selection for quality improvement and management
classified_blocks.source_text_excerpt: Use of fertilizers and manures; Protection from pests...
Protection from pests and diseases; Organic farming

classified_blocks.source_heading: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour
classified_blocks.source_text_excerpt: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour
Types of chemical reactions: combination, decomposition, displacement, double displacement, precipitation; endothermic/exothermic; oxidation/reduction
Definitions (H+ and OH- ions); indicators; neutralization; pH scale; importance of pH in everyday life; preparation/uses of Sodium Hydroxide...
Properties of metals/non-metals; Reactivity series; Ionic compounds; Metallurgical processes; Corrosion and prevention
Covalent bonds; Versatile nature of carbon; Hydrocarbons (saturated/unsaturated); Homologous series; Nomenclature...
Ethanol and Ethanoic acid (properties and uses only); soaps and detergents
Definition relating to logarithm not required for pH
Halogen, alcohol, ketone, aldehyde functional groups
Unit II: World of Living
Nutrition, respiration, transport and excretion in plants and animals
Tropic movements; plant hormones; Nervous system (Voluntary, involuntary, reflex); Chemical co-ordination...
Reproduction in animals/plants (asexual/sexual); reproductive health; family planning methods...
Heredity; Mendel's Laws of inheritance; Sex determination
Brief introduction to evolution concepts implied in heredity section
Unit III: Natural Phenomena
Reflection of light by curved surfaces; Images formed by spherical mirrors (centre of curvature...)
Refraction; Laws of refraction; Refractive index
Refraction through spherical lenses; Image formation; Lens formula and Magnification...
Functioning of human eye; defects of vision and corrections; applications of mirrors/lenses
Prism, dispersion of light, scattering of light
Excluding colour of the sun at sunrise/sunset for scattering topic
Unit IV: Effects of Current
Electric current; potential difference; Ohm's law; Resistance and Resistivity...
Series and parallel combination of resistors (and applications)
Heating effect of electric current (applications); Electric power; Interrelation between P, V, I, R
Magnetic field; Field lines; Fields due to conductors/coils/solenoids...
Direct current and Alternating current (frequency); Advantage of AC over DC; Domestic electric circuits
Unit V: Natural Resources
Eco-system; Environmental problems (Ozone depletion); Waste production and solutions
Biodegradable and non-biodegradable substances

formative_content.title: Health and Diseases (Class IX)
formative_content.source_text: The following topics are included in the syllabus but will be assessed only formatively to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments. The reduces academic stress while ensuring meaningful learning. Schools can integrate these with existing chapters as they align well.
formative_content.related_unit: Unit II: Organization in the Living World

formative_content.title: Elementary idea of conservation of Momentum (Class IX)
formative_content.source_text: The following topic is included in the syllabus but will be assessed only formatively to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments. The reduces academic stress while ensuring meaningful learning.
formative_content.related_unit: Unit III: Motion, Force and Work
formative_content.related_chapter: Force and Newton's laws / Conservation of Momentum

excluded_content: Separating the components of a mixture
Definition relating to logarithm for pH scale (Class X)
Derivation of Mirror formula and Lens formula (Class X)
Commercial unit of Energy in Work, Energy and Power section (Class IX)
Colour of the sun at sunrise and sunset (Scattering topic Class X)
Information presented in NCERT text book boxes
teacher_notes: The present syllabus has been designed around seven broad themes: Food; Materials; The World of the Living; How Things Work; Moving Things, People and Ideas; Natural Phenomenon and Natural Resources.
Curricular Goals are interdependent, not separate curricular pieces of study. (Reference: National Curriculum Framework for School Education – 2023).

The NCERT text books present information in boxes across the book. These help students to get conceptual clarity. However, the information in these boxes would not be assessed in the year-end examination.
Practicals should be conducted alongside the concepts taught in theory classes.
All practical work listed in the syllabus must be completed and maintained as a record by students.
